name: Brain CT With Contrast
description: Standard brain CT report template with intravenous contrast

findings.technique: Axial images were obtained from the skull base through the vertex following intravenous administration of iodinated contrast. Multiplanar reconstructions were performed.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Parenchyma
sections.content: The cerebral parenchyma demonstrates [normal attenuation / no hemorrhage / no mass effect / no abnormal contrast enhancement]. Gray-white matter differentiation is preserved.

sections.name: Ventricles
sections.content: The ventricular system is [normal in size / dilated / unremarkable]. No hydrocephalus identified. No abnormal ventricular enhancement.

sections.name: Midline Structures
sections.content: Midline structures are [midline / shifted]. No evidence of herniation.

sections.name: Basal Ganglia
sections.content: The basal ganglia and thalami demonstrate [normal attenuation / no abnormal enhancement].

sections.name: Vascular
sections.content: The intracranial arteries demonstrate [normal contrast opacification / no aneurysm / no stenosis / no occlusion]. The venous sinuses are patent.

sections.name: Meninges
sections.content: The meninges demonstrate [no abnormal enhancement / no dural thickening]

sections.name: Bone Windows
sections.content: The calvarium and skull base demonstrate [no lytic or blastic lesions / no fractures]. The temporal bones are [unremarkable / show mastoid air cell opacification].

impression:
1. Normal contrast-enhanced brain CT.
2. No intracranial mass, hemorrhage, or abnormal enhancement identified.
author: RadReport Templates
